reviews.review_description:
Don't get me wrong, I enjoyed this game. HOWEVER, it has zero replayablity and there were many moments I found myself looking up tutorials to figure out how to proceed to the next level/section. For example, one level expects you to search through maybe 10 different houses to find a single key. There were no hints or nods to how to find it. All I knew is there was a keyhole and I didn't have a key. The combat is a little janky but mostly consistent. It's totally fun lobbing off zombie heads, but it gets old when you've spent hours doing it. The combat is incredibly repetitive in comparison to other titles such as Blade and Sorcery.

So to end my rant
What it does well:
Dismemberment
Gore
Environment/levels
Bow and arrow (incredible, honestly)
Variety of weapons
Eating/healing (satisfying)
Physics (fantastic)
Item/weapon collision (perfect, everything collides as you expect it to)
Potions (SOO MUCH FUN)

What it does bad:
Puzzles (I felt directionless)
Combat (fun at first but it's uninspired and repetitive)
Loading screen each time you enter a house, Really??
Item/weapon storage is janky (but most VR games are like this)
Bugs (came across multiple)
Item stats (whats the point again?)

 I'll be refunding this game. I enjoyed my experience in this game but I can't see myself really ever playing it again. It wasn't bad, but it's not good enough to revisit.

I do not recommend this game. 3 stars for mediocre.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for taking the time to share such detailed feedback; we really appreciate it.

We’re glad you enjoyed elements like the physics, weapons, and atmosphere, but we also hear your concerns around replayability, puzzle clarity, and combat variety. These are areas we’re actively looking to improve, especially with upcoming updates, including multiplayer and additional content to expand the experience.

Your feedback is genuinely valuable and helps guide our improvements. Thanks again for giving the game a try.

reviews.review_description:
The game is something between Battle Talent, Behemoth and In death unchained.
The graphics are ok, the physics and the interactions with the objects are one of a kind but the fighting system is not that polished.

The gameplay gets repetitive and the variety of weapons doesn't add a lot of interest. You may spend some time seeking the only key among 10 houses or a climbing point that you didn't see. At some point, you just have to move barrels to activate switches and towards the end, you lose time just because you need to go back and restart all over just to get the fire.

Some other negative points : walking/running could be faster, no toggle for crouching, holsters are not adjustables, loading screens when entering a house.
All in all, nice game but I am happy that I didn't pay full price.

I went into this game not knowing much about it. My highlights are the graphics are awesome, the physics combat is very gratifying, and the main character is totally awesome. I think this is a fantastic game and I'm having a lot of fun with it. I would recommend this game to my buddies.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words and support! 💙
We’re really happy to hear you enjoyed both the campaign and especially Horde Mode; hearing that you still jump in for “warm-up” sessions after 10+ hours is amazing to us 😄⚔️

The intense later waves and arena design were something we put a lot of care into, so it’s great to see that resonating with players. As for the future… we can’t share anything concrete just yet 👀 but we’re still actively working on Undead Citadel, including the upcoming multiplayer update!

See you back in the Horde, Warrior 🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description:
I really enjoyed the Quest 3 update, especially the dynamic lighting and shadows. The story mode is fun to explore and really helps immerse you in the world. The gameplay itself is very solid and shines most in the horde mode.

A few notes:

1. Bug - About half the time when I select “Continue Story,” I’m met with a black screen and get stuck there. The current workaround is using level select to continue, but that becomes frustrating over time.

2. I think I’ve reached around wave 10 in the horde mode, but this highlighted why scores and stats should be recorded and easily viewable. It would be great to see things like the highest wave reached and other end-game statistics.

3. I hope more content gets added, especially enemy types, potions, and weapons. Most importantly, the enemy variety feels limited right now. While there are different enemy sizes and armor variations, many enemies share very similar attack patterns — usually a jump attack, a large turnaround wind-up swing, or a standard swing.

4. Sometimes enemies can feel a bit rubbery upon weapon collision and occasionally fly off unexpectedly.

Overall, the game is very fun, immersive, and an easy recommendation when on sale.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the detailed feedback and kind words! 💙 We’re really happy to hear you enjoyed the Quest 3 update, especially the dynamic lighting and Horde Mode experience.

We also truly appreciate the thoughtful notes:

- The black screen issue on “Continue Story” is something we’re actively investigating. Thanks for mentioning the workaround too; we know that can become frustrating over time.
- Horde statistics and progression tracking are great suggestions and would definitely make long runs more rewarding.
- More enemy variety, weapons, and gameplay depth are things we’d love to expand on moving forward.
- And yes, enemy collision/physics can occasionally behave a little too enthusiastically 😅 feedback like this really helps us refine things further.

Thanks again for the recommendation and for taking the time to break everything down so clearly. See you back in the Citadel, Warrior! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_title: Good, but I just have some complaints
reviews.review_description:
The game is pretty good with the graphics and weapons but I want these 2 changes so I hope the developers will add this

1. I think it takes too long to get new weapons, so I wish there was a cheat mode to just unlock all weapons, but you get no achievements.

2. The collisions between the weapons and enemies need some improving.

So, I hope the developers will reply with the news I'm hoping for, if true, I will be happy.

developer_response.body: Hey, please write to us at info@darkcurry.com, and someone from the team will guide you through the steps! :)

reviews.review_description:
Got this in the "sale" yesterday and gave it a whirl not having heard anything about it so went in blind.

The first thing that grabs you are the visuals which are pretty awesome. Lots of atmosphere and a very Thief-like city environment. 

The physics then are suitably weighty, you can use your sword to pick up fruit or open cupboards just like the real world.  Very immersive.

But the crown goes to the dialog of your main character which had me laughing out loud in all his rambunctiousness 😆

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words! 💙
We’re thrilled you enjoyed the atmosphere, physics, and especially the main character’s dialogue;  that means a lot to us (we had a lot of fun with him 😄).

Glad you took a chance on the game, see you back in the Citadel, Warrior! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: it is undeniably the best VR game i have ever seen

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words, it cheers us a lot! :D

reviews.review_description:
First off, entering the game it looks visually amazing, the very beginning part looks so nice and entering the citadel for the first time is such an awesome experience. Once you start to progress further, you really start to notice how linear the game is. Each level basically consists of entering an area, fighting some enemies, figuring out how to get to the next blocked off area, fight some more enemies, finish that area, open a big door, enter next level. The combat is very basic and easy, the only way you can really have "fun" with it is if you let them attack and you block it, then attack. Otherwise, you can just run up and stab them. At first, the weapons having their own stats seems pretty cool but you soon realize it means absolutely nothing because all weapons are basically the same besides its shape. The environments and graphics is what is giving it 2 stars. Otherwise, 1. I'd also like to say that all of the interiors get very bland after entering about 3 of them, and with each area you get like 5 different interiors and one of them contributes to getting to the next area. *SPOILERS*

And, by the way, when you first enter the brothel that the main character is looking for the entire time its pitch black, which is weird because every other interior is still bright and as you progress through it, he says literally nothing until you just progress to the next area and it feels so bland, like, he was just looking for this and its gonna be a big moment but you just walk through it like its another interior.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to write such a detailed review, we really appreciate the honesty and the time you put into breaking down your experience.

You’re absolutely right that Undead Citadel is a very structured, linear adventure, and we understand how that can start to feel repetitive for some players as the game progresses. The points you raise about combat variety, weapon differentiation, and interior pacing are all things we’ve seen echoed by other players as well, and they’re areas we’re actively reflecting on.

One of the reasons we’re excited about the upcoming multiplayer mode is that it directly addresses several of the concerns you mentioned. Playing alongside another warrior changes how encounters unfold — coordination, positioning, and shared chaos add new layers to combat that simply don’t exist in solo play. Even familiar environments take on a very different feel when you’re tackling them together.

We’re also using the multiplayer update as an opportunity to revisit balance, pacing, and overall engagement across the game. While multiplayer won’t change the core structure of the story, it does open the door to more dynamic and emergent moments that many players have been asking for.

Thanks again for giving the game a chance and for sharing such thoughtful feedback. Even critical reviews like this are incredibly valuable to us as we continue improving the Citadel.

reviews.review_description: This is one of my favourite games on quest and I can’t wait for multiplayer mode.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the kind words, means a lot to the whole team!

Yes, it's in works and we'll keep everyone updated :D

reviews.review_description: This game is so much fun, the combat is so cool too. Even on quest 3 the graphics are great. I totally recommend this to anyone who loves fighting games.

developer_response.body: Thank you for playing and the kind words!

reviews.review_description: The game is not what it says to be, and the graphics and combat are terrible. I bought this game thinking it would be fun, but little did I know, I was terribly off. The graphics look like a game from 2005, the weapon stats don’t do anything, and the combat style is way too easy. The maps are VERY short per level, and a lot of props look off or out of place. The voice actor scripts of the character are very weird and off. And there isn’t a lot of enjoyable content to play with. And last thing, the graphics look so bad, the textures are blurry, everything has polygons, and some of the animations are cheap. I DO NOT recommend this game to people who enjoy combat and scenery like me.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for taking the time to share your honest feedback we’re truly sorry to hear that Undead Citadel didn’t meet your expectations.

Our goal was to build a physics-driven VR experience focused on tactile combat and performance across standalone hardware, which sometimes means making visual tradeoffs to keep gameplay smooth and responsive. That said, your points about combat balance, weapon stats, environments, and presentation are completely valid, and feedback like this helps us understand where the experience didn’t land for you.

We’re actively working on updates (including multiplayer and other improvements), and we continue to refine balance, polish, and overall depth based on player input. Even when opinions are tough to read, they matter to us, so thank you for giving the game a chance and for sharing your perspective.

reviews.review_description: I just need multiplayer

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, the dev team is working hard on it, please stay tuned...

reviews.review_description:
I’ve only had VR for about a year, but the combat in this game is some of the most fun I’ve experienced so far. It feels satisfying, physical, and immersive in a way that really shows the care put into it.

Story mode genuinely keeps me on edge—especially those moments creeping through a dark house with nothing but a candle. That tension is so well done (and yeah… it got me a few times 😄).

Even though I’ve just started, I’m already excited to see where the developers take this game next, especially with multiplayer coming soon. I can’t wait to play together with my kid—VR moments like that are special.

As a player, I really appreciate the work that goes into making a game like this. I know game development isn’t easy, and it’s clear this was made by people who care about what they’re creating.


Overall:
Great experience so far. Fun combat, atmospheric moments, and a strong foundation. I’m genuinely rooting for this game and its developers and looking forward to what’s ahead.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this heartfelt review, it truly means a lot to us. 💙

We’re really happy to hear that the combat and atmosphere left such a strong impression, especially those tense candle-lit moments (they’ve scared us too 😄). Knowing that you’re excited to share future multiplayer moments with your kid is incredibly special for us. That’s exactly the kind of experience we hope to create.

Thanks again for the support and encouragement. We’re excited about what’s coming next and can’t wait to share more with you in the Citadel! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description:
The combat is great, The physics are great, and beat the game on nightmare
￼￼￼￼ overall fun ￼

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words and for this amazing feedback! Glad you're having fun in the Citadel! :D

reviews.review_description: This is one of the best games i have played on my q3. I have noticed very few glitches gameplay is great.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! 💙 We’re really happy to hear it’s become one of your favorites on Quest 3 and that the gameplay’s been treating you well. Appreciate the support, see you back in the Citadel, Warrior! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: Very fun sword and weapon physics that make you feel like there real amazing and long campaign and im exited for Multiplayer

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words, we are also looking forward to the multiplayer mode ;)

reviews.review_description: The game physics? Amazing and the voice actor guy is funny but serious at the same time you should really add him in more games and I love the ending funny yet plot twisting if your reading this get this game right now plus they have a horde mode and multiplayer is soon? Hell yeah!

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much! 💙

We’re thrilled you enjoyed the physics, the tone of the voice acting (we’ll definitely pass the love along!), and that the ending caught you off guard 😄 Hearing that you’re excited about Horde Mode and the upcoming multiplayer really means a lot to us.

Thanks for the hype and support! See you back in the Citadel very soon! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description:
First impressions were good, very atmospheric and physics felt good. I also like that the enemies are fast and you have to think quick when fighting more than one or two. 

My mediocre score is because of a few things. The map design is too linear for my liking. It would be less of a problem if the combat kept me engaged for the one hour or two I played. Already after the first opponent I discovered that decapitation was by far the best way of beating the enemies. So much so that gameplay quickly became stale, one swing and they died. Too easy to accomplish and no other combat feature ever forced me to alter my approach. 

So again, the linear design just made me feel like I was on a rollercoaster I wasn't really enjoying as much as I had hoped (I repeat, still worth the 20 dollar!). 

And I encountered no real game mechanic in the short time I played that kept me motivated. No level up system or stats to increase. All it has is its combat which unfortunately I personally wasn't a fan of.

A lot of people like the game though, so take this with a grain of salt.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thank you for the thoughtful feedback, really appreciate you taking the time to share this! 🙏

We’re glad you enjoyed the atmosphere and the intensity of the combat. Your points about progression and variety are completely fair. Undead Citadel was designed as a physics-driven action adventure first, but we’re actively exploring ways to expand gameplay depth and replayability in future updates (including the upcoming multiplayer mode 👀).

Thanks again for giving it a try and sharing such constructive feedback, it truly helps us improve! ⚔️

reviews.review_description:
Slice and dice 
light fires solve puzzles

developer_response.body: Sounds like you’re getting the full Citadel experience 😄⚔️🔥 Thanks for jumping in and sharing the love. We’re glad you’re enjoying the slicing, puzzling, and undead chaos! See you in the Citadel, Warrior!

reviews.review_description:
I got the game and doesnt load so dont get this game
since this might happen to you
I love the combat it's a bit sluggish but fun. Dismemberment is also good. The only big complaint I have is the dialog, it's really forced and just plain uncomfortable to listen to.
This game is amazing crazy graphics crazy storyline crazy horde mode but the will be crazy if they added hand tracking to the game

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words and the feedback, please keep an eye out for the multiplayer mode ;)

reviews.review_description:
It’s incredible what the developers have managed to bring to the Quest 3. Incredible physics and realtime lighting. Visuals are great and the combat is just really fun and for me it never gets old. Great atmosphere and you can interact with any object in the game. Plus the voice actor is very entertaining and believable.

I am super impressed by this game being on a standalone VR headset. It’s not a perfect game, it lacks a bit more depth with exploration but it’s definitely fun and immersive. This is a great achievement for what cans be done on standalone. It really deserves more praise in my opinion.   

Highly recommend it!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing review! 💙 We’re thrilled to hear you enjoyed the physics, lighting, atmosphere, and especially the voice acting. The team will love that! Bringing this experience to a standalone headset was a huge challenge, so it means a lot to see players appreciate the work behind it.

We hear you on wanting deeper exploration, and feedback like yours helps us keep pushing the game forward. Thanks again for the support and recommendation; see you back in the Citadel, Warrior! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: Pretty cool

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! :)

reviews.review_description:
This game slaps so hard
Exquisite game play

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the kind words, keep slaying the undead! :D

reviews.review_description: This game is alot of fun great combat perfect level of creep and action

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5-star review, we're glad you had a great time in Undead Citadel!

reviews.review_description: This is an amazing game, the intense fighting and all the different weapons, but I have only one complaint, we need spears! You have all the best weapons like bows, swords, daggers, maces, hammers, but no spears! Other than that, this game is very fun and the story is interesting. Also I like the weight mechanic and how the leverage with two handed weapons makes the damage higher. But I feel that the blocking of the enemies could be better. I feel you have to purposely hit their sword or shield to make them block, but other that that it is an amazing game, just add better enemy blocking and spears and this would make this amazing game even better.

developer_response.body:
Hey there, Warrior ⚔️

Thank you so much for the awesome feedback! We’re thrilled you enjoyed the combat, variety, and physics! Spears have definitely been one of the most requested weapons, and we’re keeping that in mind for future updates. (One of our team members has also requested it, haha)

We also appreciate your note about the enemy blocking. We will consider your feedback and do our best to improve Undead Citadel! 💪

reviews.review_description:
The physics system is definitely fun! The amount and types of weapons to play with also add a lot of variety to the game.

From your standard sword and shield all the way to dual wielding any combination of weapons. Even the handling of said weapons can be gripped differently. Then I learned you can melee/hand to hand combat and even disarm enemies. I had lots of fun boxing the undead and tearing off their limbs... just because I could!

My next favorite feature is definitely the armory, allowing you to play with all the weapons you've found and spawn enemies whenever you'd like!
I only wish you could spawn the harder enemies!

The story progression, like others, has stated that it is too direct, too linear, too rinse and repeat. Albeit not a bad story or level design, those are great! The combat just becomes too redundant. Bash, slash, or stab everything til it stops moving. It's hard to explain. However, to be fair, there are some puzzles sprinkled into the mix. That's another plus from me.

All in all, this game well deserves 5 stars! Great hero design also!

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this awesome review. We love how you captured the spirit of Undead Citadel! ⚔️💀

We’re thrilled you enjoyed the physics, weapon variety, and especially the Armory (and yes… boxing the undead never gets old 😄). Your thoughts on progression and combat variety are super valuable as we’re always looking at ways to evolve the experience, and future updates will definitely bring more depth and challenge to the fight.

Thanks again for the kind words and support, see you back in the Citadel! 🛡️🔥

reviews.review_description: It’s a must have for vr gamers. The length of the game and the challenge is perfect. I really enjoyed the whole game but there is only one exception and it’s the bow and arrows that are very difficult to aim it wasn’t bothering me most of the game and I actually liked the realism and the challenge but the level with the flaming arrows was very difficult. Finally I managed to shoot correctly but it was very frustrating. But the entire game is awesome!!!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind words. We're really glad you enjoyed the game! 🧠💥
We hear you on the bow and arrow; that section can be tricky, and feedback like this helps us fine-tune the experience. Appreciate you pushing through and sharing your thoughts! 🏹🔥

reviews.review_title: Its good u shoud get this game
reviews.review_description: K

developer_response.body: thanks for the kind words, stay tuned for the multiplayer mode :)

reviews.review_description:
Looks great, runs great. 

Didn't crash for me as other "bigger" games did (Sunshine arizona, Walking dead)

Combat is fun and responsive. Physics interactions work as you would expect.

Minor nitpick: wish there was a way to open doors without having to drop weapons. Currently you need to drop weapon, open door and quickly pick up weapon.

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for the kind words! ⚔️ We're really glad you’re enjoying the combat and performance; that means a lot to the team. 🙌

And great feedback about door interactions. It’s something we’ve heard from a few players, and we’re exploring ways to make it smoother in future updates. Thanks for playing Undead Citadel! 💀

reviews.review_title: Update – Black Screen Bug Seems Resolved
reviews.review_description:
The developer reached out to me directly, and after reinstalling the game a second time, I’ve been unable to reproduce the black screen issue I originally reported. Everything now runs smoothly, and I’ve been able to progress without any problems.

I really appreciate the developer’s responsiveness and effort to track down this bug — they took my report seriously, communicated openly, and worked on getting it fixed. Because of that, I’m increasing my rating. Very happy to say the problem seems to be resolved, and I can finally enjoy the game the way it was meant to be played!

- Greg

developer_response.body:
Hi Greg,

First off, thank you for taking the time to share such a detailed report. We completely understand your frustration. A black screen bug that blocks progression is a major issue, and we’re sorry it has interrupted your experience with Undead Citadel.

To clarify: the bug you’ve encountered has been confirmed on our side, and it’s not something we’re ignoring. The reason we mentioned timing in our earlier reply is that the fix requires a Unity engine upgrade (which is tied to other critical work we’re doing for the multiplayer update). That said, we recognize this isn’t just a minor glitch; it’s a core issue, and it deserves priority.

In the meantime, we’ve been able to reproduce the issue and are actively looking for a workaround that doesn’t force you to replay entire levels. If we can push a smaller interim patch before the major update, we absolutely will.

We truly appreciate your patience, and your feedback helps us push harder to resolve this as quickly as possible. Please know we’re listening, and we’ll keep the community updated as soon as we have progress to share.

reviews.review_description: peak

developer_response.body: Thanks for the feedback, means a lot! :)

reviews.review_description:
i just found out you can rip off arms and hit other zombies with them

 this is the best story game on quest


update

the voice acting for Sir Anvil Capheus and abbadon is spot on


Great job on this game guys!

developer_response.body: Right?! 😅 Nothing like turning a zombie’s arm into a new weapon. We’re glad you’re having a blast with the story on Quest. Thanks for jumping into the Citadel and sharing the love! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description:
This is truly a wonderful game, one of the best in the Quest Store. Almost everything about it is great: fantastic physics, an almost fully interactive environment, an amazing atmosphere, solid length and replayability.

And, importantly, the graphics are really outstanding here — probably the best lighting work I’ve ever seen in any Quest game. Yes, it features dynamic lighting, which makes some scenes genuinely eerie.

Verdict: an awesome game that’s worth every dollar. And yes, sometimes it crashes, but throughout the entire playthrough that only happened twice.

developer_response.body:
Wow, thank you so much for such a detailed and kind review! 💙 We’re happy to hear you enjoyed the physics, atmosphere, and lighting work. The team spent countless hours tuning it to make the citadel feel as eerie as possible.

Sorry about the couple of crashes you ran into. We’re closely monitoring stability and working to smooth things out further.

Hearing that you consider Undead Citadel one of the best on Quest means the world to us. Thanks for playing and sharing your experience. We’ll keep pushing to improve it! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description:
HE SAYS F_CK! In the first 5 minutes of the game, you can throw weapons and they stick in bodies and wood, 

YOU CAN USE A FORK TO KILL A ZOMBIE!!!! Arrows tracking works amazing and its dark and grimy, you can bash your head with pots
 Not sure why people are bishing but this is undoubtedly the best vr adventure game so far

Cons- no loot system, just weapons. No money either 

Overall great game and heads do pop, laughed when I slapped a zombie with another zombies arm

developer_response.body:
Haha, this made us smile, thanks for such an awesome write-up! 🤣 We’re so glad you’re enjoying the little details (yes, even zombie-arm slapstick 🧟‍♂️👉🧟‍♂️).

Totally fair on the loot system point, we focused on weapons and physics first, but feedback like yours helps us think about what could make the Citadel even deeper.

Really appreciate the love and support, and thanks for reminding us why we made this game in the first place: so players can have those “did I really just do that?!” moments. ⚔️💀

reviews.review_description: Goooooooood

developer_response.body: Thanks for the rating, please stay tuned for the multiplayer mode ;)

reviews.review_description:
This is COMPLETELY underrated! Absolutely, and amazing and groundbreaking utilization of a standalone Vr game… how?

Lighting
Physics
immersion

Then interaction of items is amazing.   Instead of just running through waves  of baddies, you can add your flare - flip tables, light candles, move around most anything..

20 bucks is way less then this title is worth - you will not be disappointed!!!!

An instant favorite from someone who has been playing VR for 5 years…get this one. You will love it…no question.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing review! 💙 

We’re thrilled you enjoyed the physics, immersion, and atmosphere, and we hear you on the holstering feedback. Really appreciate the support and recommendation! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: Greatest game i have played

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback. We are glad you had a great time! :D

reviews.review_description: I love cutting the limbs off the undead!!!

developer_response.body: And we love seeing such amazing reviews! <3

reviews.review_description: Nice escape into an interesting old world, having a great time as it progresses!

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the kind words! 🙌 We’re really happy to hear you’re enjoying the adventure and the journey deeper into the Citadel. Hope the rest of your escape is just as exciting!

See you out there, Warrior! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: One thing I want is to make an option to make it a bit brighter because I projectile Shart eveytime I go into a house

developer_response.body: We’re glad you’re enjoying the game, and totally hear you on the dark interiors; a brightness option is great feedback, and we’ll keep it in mind for future updates. Thanks for braving the spooky houses and fighting through the Citadel! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: Everything about this game was insane, the gore was great, the voice acting was actually so well made, and the game itself was amazing. Now, the puzzles were a bit confusing at first but still fun, i would recommend this for anyone looking for a great medieval fast ish paced game

developer_response.body:
Wow! Thank you so much for the amazing review! 💙 We’re happy to see you enjoyed the gore, atmosphere, and even the voice acting (our team will love hearing that!). 

Totally fair note on the puzzles, they are surely fun once you get the hang of them. Thanks for the recommendation, and we’re happy you had such a great time in the Citadel! ⚔️

reviews.review_description: If you suffer from VR nausea, avoid this game

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sharing your experience, and we’re really sorry to hear the game caused discomfort. 

VR nausea affects everyone differently, and comfort is something we always aim to improve where possible. We appreciate you giving the game a try, and we’ll continue refining comfort options so more players can enjoy the Citadel comfortably. 🙏💙

reviews.review_title: Great game ruined by final boss fight
reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: I understand the strategy on defeating the final boss. The problem is that you have to run around for 15 minutes while he destroys the pillars at random every time you die. The fact that his attacks are one shot deaths means that you will die a lot and it’s frustrating to have to go back and do the 15 minute waste of time part over and over just to fight him. Having him go straight for the 3 crystal towers would make more sense. Even in the strategy video that part is edited out and it’s till 35 minutes to defeat the boss. 

Walk around for 15 minutes doing nothing while the final boss destroys everything around you at random before you can fight him. The entire time he is spawning enemies that you can’t stop and fight because he also has a one shot lighting that will get you. In between lighting strikes he decides if he wants to blast lightning (one shot kill) or destroy a barrier that if you are unlucky enough to be standing behind you will die. You can also fall off the edge or into the pit in the middle. When you finally get to fight him your sword will break because it takes so many hits to destroy his helmet. There is no way to get a new sword unless you have a spare and you might break that one on him also. Developers didn’t really think this one out, ruined a great game.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for sharing such detailed feedback, and sorry to hear the final boss fight left you feeling more frustrated than epic. We know it can be tough, especially with the mechanics you mentioned, like weapon durability and environmental hazards.

That said, there are definitely strategies that make it more manageable (and less painful on your swords 😅). One of the community members put together a really helpful walkthrough video here that breaks down how to approach the fight:
👉 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U-NTPJTQz-E | Hopefully, you will be able to slay the final boss after going through this amazing guide and possibly change this review to ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐'s!

We’ll also take another look internally at how we can improve the balance so it feels challenging but not unfair. Really appreciate you playing through the game and sharing your thoughts, it helps us improve it. 🙏

reviews.review_description: Wow! Just a couple of minutes in and I love the atmosphere and physics in this game. The attention to detail is amazing! Even the individual candlesticks can be lit. The dismemberment of the opponents is done PERFECTLY compared to other sword games! Well done devs!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the awesome feedback! 💙 We’re really glad you’re enjoying the atmosphere, physics, and all those little details (lighting candles never gets old 😄). Hearing that the dismemberment stands out compared to other sword games means a lot to our team. Thanks for jumping into the Citadel and for the kind words! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: Very good lighy rpg great to practice combat before enjoying the nice story mode

developer_response.body: Thank you for the kind words! Much appreciated! :)

reviews.review_title: I love this game so much
reviews.review_description: 5 stor

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, we are glad you had a great time playing our game! 💙

reviews.review_title: I wish I could give it 4.5 stars
reviews.review_description: Great game. It’s fun and the mechanics are great. A small glitch here or there but they did a good job on it. HOWEVER, the final boss fight is super hard it is too hard. If you look it up online or on YouTube, a lot of people rage quit or give up. I hope they make another dungeon crawler but don’t make the final boss ￼so terribly difficult.

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks so much for the kind words. We’re happy you enjoyed the game and had fun with the combat! 🙌

We hear you on the final boss. We wanted it to feel like a real challenge, but we know it’s pushed some players to the edge (and sometimes off it 😅). We’re already taking in feedback like yours for future updates and projects, so this helps.

reviews.review_description: rip and tear until it is done

reviews.review_description: Don’t get me wrong the gore is good but will there be slice wounds and stab wounds at all that’s all I think is missing and it way to easy to dismember a enemy also deathl animations could be more brutal but the game is pretty good I just wish there where slice wounds and stab wounds and will there be mid support at but good game just thought of this mesh deformation

developer_response.body: We totally hear you, slice/stab wounds and more brutal deaths would be awesome additions, and mod support’s something we’d love to explore down the line. Appreciate the feedback! ⚔️🧟‍♂️

reviews.review_description: Hello this is the best game ever! I love i can't wait for the next huge update im going to tell you guys what I love to be in the game so pls make it where blood can get on your screen that would be amazing and realistic and make blood splats in the game it would make it better and thats it tysm for this amazing game! Can't  WAIT for the new update I'm subbed to you're channel anddd im on your discord!

I also get the black screen with audio but no picture. I have also encounter during the 2nd part when you pick up the potion off the shelf it will freeze and I can turn in circles but not move or anything else. I was loving the story but I had to get a refund.

Update: devs reached out personally to figure out the issues. I got past the bug on level 2 and the game os insanely fun and amazing. 4 stars just because of that bug on part 2.

developer_response.body:
Hey, firstly, thank you for updating your review, we really appreciate it!

To be fully transparent, the new update we’re working on requires a Unity engine upgrade, which will take some time as we’re also developing the multiplayer update in parallel.

Rest assured, the issue will be fixed within the next couple of months. We’re also investigating why it suddenly appeared after months of stability, but we’re committed to resolving it.

Our sincerest apologies if this affected your in-game experience, we’ll make sure it’s fixed in the next update! :)

reviews.review_description: best game ever

reviews.review_description: Oh god this game is great… the slicing, the enemies, the weapons all the way to its environment looks great. You feel like a true warrior while slicing up the enemies whether it’s skeletons or zombies. The animations are smooth and those big enemies that appear as if they were a mini boss fight make me go all excited and energetic that I feel immersed within the world of the game. The fact that it’s in early access still means it’s going to get more and better updates making this game unstoppable, I’m mostly excited for the multiplayer update which is gonna be fire 🔥. Keep it up!!!

developer_response.body: Hello there, thank you! We are still working on the Multiplayer Update and just like you - we are really excited as well!

reviews.review_title: Very good game but some issues.
reviews.review_description: The game has the best combat in my opinion. better than Blade and sorcery Nomad combat. Can use some fixings but other than that it's really good. One of The issues I found was that when you dismembered the enemy's arms they can hit you with nothing for an arm so it seemed like they were hitting you with air so can you please make animations for the legs and head so it seems like you are getting hit with something instead of nothing. The other issue is that bows don't work correctly like it works but it doesn't hit the target because the arrow would move to the left or the right before hitting the target.

Hello sleepdepraved629, thank you so much for the review & feedback.

We'll make sure to go into the details of your report regarding the Bows and melee hits, appreciate it!

reviews.review_description:
When loading the "rubble" level it just crashes. Checked for updates, uninstalled, etc. Still crashes at the same loading screen. 

Not my headset, every other game runs fine and im up to date on os updates. Previously gave the game 5 stars but game breaking crashes kind of a deal breaker. 

This seems to be happening more often. Makes wonder if Meta and their corporate lizard king overlord is performing "planned obsolescence" on quest 2 headsets. Which is messed up bc i bought this from meta online store new, 2 years ago. 

Either way be warned, this game has game breaking crashes for whatever reason.

We will investigate this issue as soon as possible.

developer_response.body:
Hey there, we're glad to hear that you enjoyed playing the game. We have also noted your feedback regarding the curse words and are truly sorry for the inconvenience it may have caused you.

For the time being, can you please try to play the game with subtitles off and at a lower volume to avoid any troubles for you and your friend?  We'll let the devs know about the "clean option" and see what we can do about the curse words in a future update. 

About the arms, we'll have something for that in our next big update, shhhhhhhhh! ;)

I'm at the part where im pretty sure you have to find a torch to burn the rubble and rags that reek oil down. Other than that I love the game and keep working hard!

And you are right, you can find the torch in one of the houses to burn down the rubble.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely love this game and im only at the start of game. What I can say is Graphics are good Mechanics are amazing and I would recommend playing. I would say that this game is like Blade n Sorcery and Saint's n Sinner's had a baby. If you liked either of those then you will love Undead Citadel. I gave 5 stars 🤩

reviews.review_description:
Started to play today. Have already a few hours played it. I am now playing story mode, easy level. 
You just have to kill (again?) the undead ones. Search in houses for food (mostly cheese, bread and apples 😹) to get your health back up.

Your mission is to get to the citadel. On your way, undead people want you  dead. Kill them before they get you. 
Easy. 

Graphics of the game are very good and realistic. Storyline is great. Also a good workout when a group of undeads comes to get you…

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a review, we really appreciate it, and happy that you had fun!

reviews.review_description: You all need this game it has very great combat the the undeads react to where you hit them it’s perfect this is better than hellsplit

reviews.review_title: This game is great some minor complaints
reviews.review_description: This game outclasses blade and sorcery on combat and story, but I wish the story mode was longer and there was like a type of euphoria engine, Thats all. This is really fun to record and edit videos of this game!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review, and the feedback  - we really appreciate it nubeo_9! ❤️

reviews.review_title: Good undead brawler - with a few issues
reviews.review_description: It's a pretty good undead brawler with lots of free form physics based action. You can really carve these zombies up and its fun to see the pieces go flying.  If that's the genre you're looking for, you'll like this game. Graphics are nice on Quest 3 with the torch lighting update and shadows, and the BHaptics vest support was great.  Wish all games had those features.  My criticisms are more on the smaller side of things, starting with a lack of spatial audio. Lots of times enemies will be moving about and the audio will sound like it comes from right on top of you rather than their actual position, resulting in your hearing being unreliable.  Additionally, sometimes it is hard to know where to go, as the game often requires a key that could be hidden in a random nondescript house and is lacking in guidance cues.  Further, one handed weapons feel great, but the two handed ones I found to just be unwieldy and I could never use them effectively.  Finally, the boss fight at the end seems overly laborious.  The one hit KO attacks, combined with the slow nature of waiting for him to hit all the crystals is at odds with the proactive nature of the rest of the games combat, leading to a bit of a slog at the end of a fun brawler.  Overall a good game, but with some issues.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the feedback! We actually received the same kind of review from many of our players and decided to provide a bit more guidance on what to do next, or at least offer some hints to the players. Some of those hints are already in the game, but it looks like we can use more!

Appreciated!

reviews.review_title: Pretty good, but still room to improve
reviews.review_description:
It is a real good game with story and most of the objects have psychics, I loved the fact that I picked up a barrel and used it as a shield and made my way towards an archer , it can feel quite spooky when you enter in a house and the only light comes from a fire and you know there are undead inside, my only complain about the game is the combat , I feel there could be more done over there, I would love to
 feel some vibrations from my controller when I hit the enemy and sometimes when I hit them I feel like I m pushing them away , I would also love some improvements when it comes to sound when I fight , when you hit them it can feel very silent .
Overall a good game with good story 
4.5/5

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback and the review! We keep working hard for the best possible experience.

reviews.review_title: Good, but needs to step back a bit
reviews.review_description: I think this game is amazing, just a few small issues. Difficulty: The game feels either really easy or impossibly difficult. It's very easy to slice an enemy in half and call it a day. (Also, the quips can get repetitive but are really good) Puzzles: Some don't even work. For example, pressure plate puzzels very rarely glitch to make the puzzle impossible. Or just difficult with how dark the world gets later on ( i play on Quest 2, so i won't talk about graphics, but they are pretty solid on what its meant to run on). Some are fun, and others fall kinda short. Exploration: while I like the ability to go inside, I think it makes keys really hard to find, and makes levels harder and longer, normally each levels about 10 minutes, but with a bad puzzle or hidden item it makes it 30. You also sometimes have to go back and forth just to do a puzzle (see final level). Overall, combats fun, power ups are amazing, and the boss is good. I also think horde mode could use some fixing. Even though I've been negative in this review, this game is amazing, and if you liked Blade and Sorcery, you'll enjoy this game even more.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback The7seas! You mentioned 'some of the puzzles don't event work' and we would love to go deeper on this. Please join our Discord below and tell us about I!

-> https://discord.gg/undeadcitadel

reviews.review_title: It’s fun but only for a day or two
reviews.review_description:
I’m just gonna list the things I struggled with and disagree on

1.its more of a Ragdoll simulator than an actual game
2. You never fight enough enemy’s you see the trailer and think its gonna be a horde type game
3.enemies do too much damage and just fly back to their feet when knocked over even on easy mode
4.they shouldn’t just fall to the ground from my fists. They need stability and more realistic getting up moves
5.i didn’t know where to go 4 different times in my 3 hours of gameplay. I was lost and had to watch walkthroughs.
6.you can box tf out of the final boss and he can’t do a thing, as god intended.
7.not enough content, seems more like a puzzle game weirdly.
8.archers are op asf. But when you get close they stop trying to shoot you and box, also they fire an extra shot when you get close.
9.no replayability. I buy games only when I know their is value like blade and sorcery with its mod adaptability and sandbox mode. But after 3 hours horde mode is all you have and it just doesn’t feel as good
10.i don’t think this game is worth what I paid for. Can’t refund it because when I went to bed my headset never turned off and thus I passed my 2 hours. This is a good $10 maybe $15 game but other than that you shouldn’t bother.

developer_response.body:
Hello there, thank you for the review and feedback!

We have 2 game modes, one is Campaign and the other one is the Horde Mode - where you fight with endless waves of enemies. You will definitely find enough enemies to fight there! We are also working on the Multiplayer Mode, more news on this soon!

When it comes to 'clues' or navigation during gameplay, we heard this from our player base, and currently we are working on it to give you a clear idea of what to do next, especially at the early acts of the game. 

We have many different weapons in the game, so for replayability, you can always try to find them to use in the Horde Mode. Speaking of Horde Mode, it's also a game mode you can try for replayability with different weapons & strategies. But we hear you on Sandbox Mode! As I mentioned before, we are also working on Multiplayer Mode, which should give you a lot of replayability! 

Hopefully, we can see you in the Citadel again in the future!

reviews.review_description: Jeez, it’s an amazing game. I didn’t like it that much on the Quest 2 but on the Quest 3 it’s a whole new experience!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! ❤️

reviews.review_description: I both like this game and blade an sorcery and please add when stabb please blood but five stars like it splashes all over please

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! We are fighting against the skeletons after all, we might need to think further about blood! :P

reviews.review_description:
No diss or smack talk. This game is fun and idk if its because I can search buildings or if its the combat but this is a ton of fun.
Looking for a melee focused adventure this is a fun time. I am not a robot.

developer_response.body: Thank you, St.Potatos! Exploration is a part of the Undead Citadel experience, and we are happy that you had fun with it! ❤️

reviews.review_description: Excellent graphics once you increase the resolution in Quest Games Optimizer, a very good map of a half-destroyed village, fantastic physics of objects and enemies.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much, hope you enjoyed your adventure!

reviews.review_description: most fun destructive combat i’ve played in a while. the time potion is the best. sir anvil is a very good funny character, when he said “f*ck you demon” it made my day. only 4 stars though because i keep losing rare weapons from them gliching when i accidentaly pick them up trying to climb walls

You can smash up pots and knock over shelves and flip over tables there so much physics in this game that it's mind boggling it runs so good on a quest 3! And the combat is incredible like you can slice off limbs and the enemies will adapt to having no arms or no legs and once they're dead you can hack up their bodies but they're all undead skeletons so you don't feel bad. As for people saying it's too dark you can light candles from other things on fire. The story mode is really good but if you just want to hack and slash enemies there's a horde mode that will put you into combat immediately.

developer_response.body:
You can also use those shelves or chairs as a shield! Just a little tip. :P

Thank you so much for the kind words and the detailed review!

reviews.review_description: Well I have to say the game is very, very good but one thing that really annoys me is that you don't have a body at all, of course it's not that bad at the same time but I think there should be an option where you have a whole body or just flying hands. I personally like full body because then you feel like you're playing the character.

developer_response.body:
Thanks so much for the great review and the thoughtful feedback! 💙 We’re really glad you’re enjoying the game overall.

You’re absolutely right, having the option between floating hands and a full body can really affect immersion, and it’s something we’ve talked about internally too. While it’s not in the current build, feedback like yours helps us shape future updates and priorities.

Appreciate you taking the time to share your perspective, we’ll keep it in mind as we continue improving Undead Citadel! ⚔️🧟‍♂️
